steps.title: Identify the rhetorical goal: contrast the two samples
steps.content: The student wants to CONTRAST the two samples — to highlight what differs between them, not what they have in common or general facts about torsional heating.

steps.title: Match the data to that goal
steps.content: Choice A directly contrasts the samples by identifying a concrete difference: the natural rubber sample (2.2 millimeters) is thicker than the NiTi wire (0.7 millimeters). Choices B, C, and D fail to contrast—B emphasizes what's similar (both heated), C explains torsional heating in general, and D merely names the samples without comparing them. That matches choice A — A is correct.
